← the Atlas

Remote terminals — the finale (R9–R10)

budding·proposed·JUN 29, 2026

The R9–R10 plan of record. R8's reader-join stays. The awareness architecture for remote terminals is SETTLED in the awareness design note — split observing from remembering. A memoryless producer per host emits Observation events, and kolu alone folds them, owns the memory (on kolu.authored), and stamps recency with its own clock. Two earlier directions are dead — the local-pulam-PROCESS (#1614, closed) and createPulam / share-the-assembly (superseded). So R9·lib dissolves. Phases — S1 producer · S2 kolu-fold · R9.0 in-process · R9.1 resolver (shipped) · R9.2 ssh PTY · R9.3 remote subscribe-and-fold · R9.4 reconnect · R9.5 Code-tab fs/git · R10 canvas.

Contents

  • Roadmap
  • What R9 reuses — and what it must build
  • The sub-phases
    • S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation, and why R9·lib dissolves
    • R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds
    • R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped
    • R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile
    • R9.3 — remote awareness = subscribe the remote event stream + the same fold
    • R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption
    • R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • Open decisions

The last leg of #951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8 (#1594) shipped the right reader-join (a terminal is kolu.authored ⋈ terminalWorkspace.awareness, joined at the reader) — keep that. The question this note settles is how kolu gets the awareness half for a remote host, and that architecture is now decided: split observing from remembering. A memoryless producer runs beside each host’s kaval and emits Observation events; kolu alone folds them, owns the memory (on kolu.authored), and stamps recency with its own clock. The full types/API/flows are in the awareness design; this note is the phase plan of record. The parent remote-terminals keeps the one-line rows; the model is in the terminal model.

The settled design — and the two directions it replaced

The awareness boundary is observe vs. remember (the awareness design, outcome of a grounded 3-agent debate). It supersedes two earlier attempts, both now dead: (1) the local-pulam-PROCESS (#1614, CLOSED) — it conflated a process boundary with code decoupling and introduced a fold-clobber intrinsic to mirroring an ephemeral process; (2) createPulam / “share the assembly” — it shared the wrong thing (the assembly still fused deriving with storing, so the foot-gun and clobber rode along to every consumer). The converged fix doesn’t share the assembly; it deletes it: the producer is memoryless (it can’t even spell memory, by type), so there is nothing to clobber and nothing to assemble. R9·lib therefore dissolves (below).

Roadmap

FINALE — R9 (converged) · R10✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
○S1 · memoryless awareness producer — emits observations, no seed, Observation onlythe foundation · → awareness design
○S2 · kolu's fold owns memory — typed patches · kolu's clock · memory→authored · event streamneeds S1
✕R9·lib · DISSOLVES — createPulam unneeded; producer + fold are pure leavessubsumed by S1+S2
○R9.0 · kolu runs the producer in-process, folds onto authored + awarenessneeds S2
✓R9.1 · the TerminalEndpoint resolver (orthogonal — shipped)#1603
○R9.2 · ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tileneeds R9.1
○R9.3 · remote awareness = subscribe the remote event stream + the SAME foldneeds S2 · R9.2 · R7
○R9.4 · persisted location + reconnect + adoptionneeds R9.2
○R9.5 · Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulseneeds R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3
○R10 · canvas multiplex + validateneeds R9.5

S1 + S2 are the foundation — the memoryless producer and kolu’s fold. Once they land, R9·lib dissolves (nothing to assemble), R9.0 is kolu running the producer in-process and folding, and R9.3 is kolu subscribing a remote producer’s event stream into the same fold — no separate reconcile. R9.1 shipped (the PTY-endpoint path, orthogonal to awareness). The PTY/transport phases (R9.2 · R9.4 · R9.5 · R10) are unchanged by the awareness redesign.

What R9 reuses — and what it must build

Shipped seams (reuse as-is):

seam where leg it serves
the per-terminal sensors @kolu/terminal-workspace the derivation S1 turns into a memoryless producer (git · pr · agent · foreground)
the pulam daemon pulam (R4) the one producer per host — now serving an observation stream, kept for the remote case
frontDaemonOverStdio @kolu/surface-daemon dial a remote kaval over ssh stdio → the remote PTY
getHostSession @kolu/surface-nix-host a pooled, long-lived session to dial a remote producer → its event stream
mirrorRemoteSurface @kolu/surface the total dual ({ procedures, done }, R7) — mirror a remote producer’s stream into one local handle
HostLocation remote arm · persisted location @kolu/common { kind:"remote", hostId }, round-trips to disk
the reader-join (composeTerminalMetadata) @kolu/common · client authored ⋈ awareness at read + at disk — unchanged (memory just moves to the authored half)

Must build:

gap today the phase
make the sensors a memoryless producer (observations, no seed, Observation) the engine mutates a host record + reads it back (sensors.ts:139-162) S1
kolu’s fold owns the gates + the clock; memory moves to kolu.authored; an awarenessEvents stream kolu/daemon each hand-roll a sink that writes the store S2
kolu runs the producer in-process and folds kolu wires startAwareness against a registry-aliased record R9.0
the ssh driver (the remote PTY arm) only {kind:"local"} exists R9.2
subscribe a remote producer’s event stream into kolu’s fold — R9.3

The sub-phases

The awareness phases (S1 · S2 · R9.0 · R9.3) are specified in full — types, API, interaction diagrams — in the awareness design; their one-liners are below. The PTY/transport phases (R9.1 · R9.2 · R9.4 · R9.5 · R10) are detailed here.

S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation, and why R9·lib dissolves

S1· memoryless awareness producer — observations, no seed, Observation only○ todo
needs ← R6 · R4
S2· kolu's fold owns memory — kolu's clock, memory→authored○ todo
needs ← S1

Converged + landed. The S1/S2 model below was simplified across a long review and shipped by the awareness-derive-store cutover — read that note for the final types. The summary here is updated to match: the three-category {cache, live, memory} patch model and the per-event fromSnapshot flag were dropped (snapshot-vs-delta rides the frame, not the event).

S1 rewrites the sensors into a memoryless producer: it emits per-field AwarenessObservation events, holds no durable memory, takes no seed, and its export type Observation (the five re-observable fields) cannot spell the memory fields. It deletes the AwarenessSink, both makeAwarenessSink impls, the AwarenessRecord, and the apply-and-publish read-back contract. S2 gives kolu the only fold: fold(cur, o, ctx) → KoluAwareness ({observed, memory}), last-write-wins for the five observed fields, stamping recency with kolu’s clock on a live agent-identity change and remembering lastAgentCommand (the resume target rides the authored record as the fold-derived discriminated restoreTarget). It writes memory onto kolu.authored — so every awareness collection everywhere is Observation.

Why R9·lib dissolves: createPulam was an attempt to share the assembly the daemon and kolu each hand-rolled. Under S1+S2 there is no assembly — the producer and the fold are pure leaves, each home’s loop is ~8 lines, and the producer can’t write memory, so there is nothing to clobber and nothing to consolidate. The R9·lib / createPulam phase is removed.

  • Needs: R6 (the sensor library) and R4 (the pulam daemon) — both shipped.
  • Done (S1): the producer emits observations and touches no host store; AwarenessSink/makeAwarenessSink×2/AwarenessRecord/seed are gone. Done (S2): kolu’s fold is the sole writer of memory (onto kolu.authored); the write-fence, clock authority, and single-memory-writer are enforced by type (tests in the awareness note). The remote awarenessEvents wire stream is R9.3; R9.0 folds the in-process producer and pulam serves an Observation collection.

R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds

R9.0· kolu runs the memoryless producer in-process and folds○ todo
needs ← S2

kolu’s local awareness is the memoryless producer called in-process (no daemon, no socket, no mirror), its observations fed straight into kolu’s fold. kolu seeds current from its durable record, folds with its clock, writes the memory fields to kolu.authored and the rest to the awareness collection. The reader-join is unchanged (composeTerminalMetadata now reads memory from the authored half). Clobber-free by type (the producer can’t spell memory).

  • Migration: a hard cutover, no flag/fallback; plus the memory→kolu.authored schema move (SCHEMA_VERSION bump + state migration).
  • Needs: S2.
  • Done: kolu runs no bespoke sink/assembly; local awareness is the producer + kolu’s fold; the reader-join and disk persistence hold; a fold differential test passes. Detail: the awareness design.

R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped #1603

R9.1· HostLocation-keyed endpoint resolver✓ shipped
needs ← R8links #1603

The PTY-endpoint path — orthogonal to awareness, so the redesign doesn’t touch it. resolveTerminalEndpoint is the one HostLocation-keyed seam mapping a terminal to its kaval endpoint; the {kind:"remote"} arm fails loudly until R9.2 fills it (.exhaustive() forces a deliberate arm). Per-terminal ops resolve off entry.meta.location, so R9.2 is a one-case addition.

R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile

R9.2· ssh kaval driver — remote PTY○ todo
needs ← R9.1

Add the {kind:"remote",hostId} arm to the resolver: dial the remote host’s kaval over frontDaemonOverStdio (the same ssh <host> kaval --stdio relay kaval-tui --host uses) → a remote PTY on the canvas, PTY only (no awareness badge yet). Persist location = {kind:"remote",hostId}. Adoption reuses the local boot’s adoptOrEnsure spine + the pid-gate’s atomic link(2) (a dial joins a host’s kaval, never races to kill it).

  • Needs: R9.1.
  • Done: dial a host, spawn a PTY, see an ssh-badged remote tile that echoes; it survives a network blip via re-attach. e2e: a remote PTY round-trips over ssh.

R9.3 — remote awareness = subscribe the remote event stream + the same fold

R9.3· remote awareness — subscribe the remote producer's event stream○ todo
needs ← S2 · R9.2 · R7

A remote host runs the same memoryless producer (in its pulam daemon), serving an ordered awarenessEvents stream of Observation. kolu subscribes over a long-lived getHostSession (mirrored via R7’s total dual) and folds those events through the same fold it uses locally — kolu seeds current from its durable record, stamps recency with its clock, writes memory to kolu.authored. Because kolu seeds from memory and the stream is snapshot-then-deltas, the snapshot frame is the reconcile — there is no separate reconcileRemoteSnapshot. Only the transport differs (in-process vs ssh); the fold, the reader-join, and disk persistence are identical.

  • Needs: S2 (the producer + fold), R9.2 (the remote PTY), R7 (the total dual).
  • Done: a remote tile shows live awareness folded from the remote producer’s stream, via the same fold as local; a differential test pins it equal to the producer’s observed state. Detail: the awareness design.

R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption

R9.4· persisted location + reconnect + adoption○ todo
needs ← R9.2

Harden the remote lifecycle: re-validate the persisted location on reconnect, and cover the adoption/reconcile cases.

the remote kaval is… kolu does
absent provision + spawn fresh (adoptOrEnsure → ensure)
live · wire-compatible adopt — connect, never kill; its PTYs reconcile in
live · a build behind adopt + an “update pending” nudge
live · contract-skewed recycle (kill → respawn) — only on a typed DaemonContractSkewError
unreachable bounded retry, then degraded — never killed
  • Open (decide here): what is re-validated on reconnect and the fail behavior. location is the host discriminator (kolu.authored); cwd is an observed field — different homes. Spell out the comparison and the fail-fast (crash loudly vs re-derive); don’t invent it at implementation time.
  • Needs: R9.2.
  • Done: a kolu-server restart re-adopts remote PTYs; each adoption-table row has a test.

R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)

R9.5· Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulse○ todo
needs ← R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3

The one large client change, and the only finale phase R-pulamweb-4 gates. The Code tab reads koluSurface’s value-bearing streams today (CodeTab.tsx — app.streams.gitStatus/fsListAll/gitDiff.use). Rewrite it to the shared surface’s procedure + {seq} pulse: call the procedure once, re-query on each pulse (the two wire shapes are in surface live data). This deletes the last koluSurface fs/git reads and works for local and remote tiles alike.

  • Needs: R-pulamweb-4 (proves the procedure + pulse remote leg over websocketLink + surfaceClient), R9.3.
  • Done: the Code tab shows live git status/diff for a remote tile via procedure + pulse; no koluSurface fs/git reads remain.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← R9.5

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front.

  • Needs: R9 (all of it).
  • Done: the validated UX call, recorded.

Open decisions

The awareness forks the debate raised are decided (recorded in the awareness design): memory home = kolu.authored; recency = identity-only (the restore-caveat heuristic deleted); dashboards = urgency-only; aggregation key = (HostLocation, TerminalId); the activity-definition predicate is fixed in one tested function. The only finale decision still open:

decision needed by options
reconnect re-validation semantics R9.4 what’s compared (location vs the remote kaval’s live cwd) + the fail-fast behavior

Referenced by

  • Awareness — the producer observes, kolu remembers
  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • The Terminal Model — two collections, one reader-side join (and what to name them)
